task_completion.description: Verify the agent modified the configuration to securely grant Docker socket access to the non-root user
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing edits to /home/ga/build-agent/docker-compose.yml (e.g., adding 'group_add' with the host's docker GID) or Dockerfile (e.g., creating a docker group with the host's GID and adding the 'agent' user to it).

task_completion.description: Verify the build-agent container is successfully running
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of 'docker ps' showing the 'build-agent' container with a status of 'Up', or 'docker-compose up -d' completing successfully.

task_completion.description: Verify the host's Docker socket permissions were not changed to world-writable
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of 'ls -l /var/run/docker.sock' or 'stat /var/run/docker.sock' on the host showing permissions like 'srw-rw----' (not 'srwxrwxrwx' or 777).

task_completion.description: Verify the container is still running as the 'agent' user (UID 1000)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of 'docker exec build-agent id' or 'docker exec build-agent whoami' showing 'uid=1000(agent)' or 'agent'.

task_completion.description: Verify 'docker ps' executes successfully inside the container
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of the exact command 'docker exec build-agent docker ps' executing successfully and displaying the standard Docker container list headers (CONTAINER ID, IMAGE, COMMAND, etc.) without any 'permission denied' errors.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the security requirement by making the host socket world-writable
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the terminal history to ensure commands like 'chmod 777 /var/run/docker.sock' or 'chmod a+rw /var/run/docker.sock' were NOT executed on the host.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the non-root requirement by running the container as root
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that 'user: root' was NOT added to docker-compose.yml, and 'USER agent' was NOT removed or changed to 'USER root' in the Dockerfile.

integrity.description: Ensure the success outputs are from actual command execution
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the outputs for 'docker exec build-agent docker ps' appear as standard stdout in the terminal, not as text written in an editor or printed via 'echo'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is '/home/ga/build-agent'. The container name is 'build-agent'. The target user inside the container must remain 'agent' with UID 1000. The host's docker socket is mounted to the container. Success requires the container to run as the non-root 'agent' user, the host's /var/run/docker.sock permissions to remain unchanged (not world-writable), and the command 'docker exec build-agent docker ps' to execute successfully.
